title.zh: 人面虎身、啼声如婴的食人兽
title.en: Man-Eater with a Human Face, a Tiger's Body, and an Infant's Cry

summary.zh: 蔓渠山食人异兽，人面虎身，叫声却像婴儿啼哭，闻声近前的人就成了它的猎物。伊水从它盘踞的山中发源。
summary.en: A man-eating beast of Mount Manqu with a human face on a tiger's body, whose cry mimics a wailing infant; the Yi River rises from its mountain.

description.zh:
《中次二经》记蔓渠之山“其上多金玉，其下多竹箭”，伊水由此发源，东流注入洛水。山中有兽名马腹，“其状如人面虎身，其音如婴儿，是食人”：一张人脸安在虎躯上，开口却是婴儿的啼哭，靠这声啼哭把人引到近前吞吃。

用婴儿啼声作诱饵的食人兽在《山海经》里不止一头：南山经青丘山的九尾狐“其音如婴儿，能食人”，同在伊洛流域的犀渠也是“其音如婴儿，是食人”。最柔弱无害的声音配上最凶暴的胃口，是这部书写猛兽的惯用配方。

人面兽身的形象在书中自成谱系：昆仑山神陆吾虎身九尾而人面虎爪，守昆仑门户的开明兽身大类虎而九首皆人面。马腹没有它们的神职，只在伊水源头做一头凶兽。
description.en:
From Mount Manqu, rich in gold and jade above and arrow-bamboo below, rises the Yi River, which flows east into the Luo. The mountain harbors the Mafu, a beast with a human face set on a tiger's body. Its voice is that of a crying infant, and it eats people: the helpless sound draws would-be rescuers within reach of the claws.

The infant-cry lure is a recurring device among the man-eaters of the Shanhaijing. The nine-tailed fox of Mount Qingqiu in the southern mountains wails like a baby and devours people, and the Xiqu, an ox-shaped man-eater of the same Yi-Luo basin, uses the identical call.

Human-faced beasts form their own lineage in the book. Lu Wu, warden of Kunlun, bears a tiger's body, nine tails, a human face and tiger claws; the Kaiming beast guarding Kunlun's gates carries nine human faces. The Mafu holds no such office. It simply hunts at the source of the Yi.

image.sourceTitle: Imperial Encyclopaedia - Animal Kingdom - pic297 - 馬腹圖
